name: Fasten your seatbelts
description: This project was about creating an embedded system using an Odroid. Together with a team of students we designed an air hockey table with an autonomous opponent. Through the use of random movements the robot armed opponent would knock back the puck. We worked using Python and used a kit of sensors and various components.
contribution: I was responsible for the software development of the robot. I programmed the robot to move in random directions and to hit the puck. I also worked on the communication between the robot and the Odroid.

name: PAD - NAO Robot
description: Worked with a team of students to create an entertaining fitness routine for elderly people. The NAO robot would guide the user through a series of exercises. The project was done in Java and Android Studio. We also used the Choregraphe software to program the robot.
contribution: I was responsible for the software development of the Android application. I programmed the application to communicate with the NAO robot and to display the exercises. I also worked on the user interface.

name: Robot on Wheels
description: This project was about creating a 'Robot on Wheels' using an Raspberry Pi. The code was written in C++ and we used OpenCV for the computer vision. The concept for our robot was an Elephant robot that could automatically recognize plants and be able to water them through it's trunk.
contribution: I worked on computer vision through Aruco markers, the robot would be able to tell where a plant is and drive to it. I worked with various sensors, and worked on making Android app that could communicate with the robot through MQTT commands.

name: IoT Game console
description: For my time following the minor Internet of Things I created a game console. The system consists of 2 micro controller, one for the wireless controller and one for the console. All code written in C++ using PlatformIO.
contribution: I was responsible for everything from developing the game to designing the 3D models. I also worked on the communication between the controller and the console I went through some iterations trying different protocols until being satisfied with using the ESP-NOW protocol.

name: A Sol's Journey
description: Entry in the PirateSoftware - Game Jam 15 with the theme Shadows & Alchemy. The game is a 2D topdown shooter where you play as a little soul named appropiately named Sol. It's a small demo showing some abilities and shooting mechanis ending with a mini boss. The game was developed in a few days together with 2 other students.
contribution: I was responsible for most parts of the game including the art and coding the game mechanics. The other teammates unfortunately didn't have as much time to dedicate to the project at the time.

name: Alchimea Calibration - VBH Internship
description: The alchimea project by Van Berge Henegouwen is consists of many products that are hidden in the environment.
contribution: During my internship I worked on creating a calibration tool for the Alchimea display system. I worked on graphics with Three.js, web development with Typescript, CAD for PCB creation and 3D printing and embedded programming through the Arduino library and C++.
